headline: Why Is United Rentals (URI) Stock Rocketing Higher Today
summary: Shares of equipment rental company United Rentals (NYSE:URI) jumped 12.1% in the afternoon session after the company reported second-quarter results that beat Wall Street's top and bottom-line estimates and raised its full-year guidance. Adjusted earnings per share came in at $12.76, topping the consensus estimate of $11.59. Revenue grew 11.8% year-over-year to $4.41 billion, surpassing expectations of $4.20 billion. headline: S&P 500 Earnings: URI Flashes Buy Signal As Google Hikes Capex
summary: S&P 500 member United Rentals soared on stronger-than-forecast Q2 results after Wednesday's close as Google got a late-day haircut.  AI data center construction has helped supercharge earnings for URI, which provides a one-stop shop for large construction projects.  Google's plan to keep boosting capital spending suggests the demand environment for heavy machinery will keep shining, even as it may slow the pay-off for AI hyperscalers.

headline: Lockheed Martin, Quest Diagnostics, Thermo Fisher Scientific And Other Big Stocks Moving Higher On Thursday
summary: U.S. stocks were down as Dow Jones fell 450 points on Thursday. Lockheed Martin (LMT) shares rose 11.4% on better-than-expected results. Other big gainers were NVE Corp (NVEC), Gibo Holdings Ltd (GIBO), Novocure Ltd (NVCR), Cleveland-Cliffs Inc (CLF), Merlin Inc (MRLN), Medpace Holdings Inc (MEDP), Oatly Group AB (OTLY), Imax Corp (IMAX), United Rentals Inc (URI), Oceaneering International Inc (OII), and Quest Diagnostics Inc (DGX).
